english: revered by students of medicine, and frequently invoked in medicinal writings, as well as in medical practice, v. Schl. p. 266 sqq. (sman-gyi lha (sman gyi lha) Glr., is prob. but a misprint). — Other compounds: sman-rkyál (sman rkyal) medicine-bag, smaller or larger leather-bags being the usual receptacles for the commodities of grocers and the drugs of physicians. — smán-k'aṅ (sman khang) apothecary's shop. — sman-k'úg (sman khug) medicine-bag. — sman-sgá (sman sga) a kind of officinal ginger (?) S.g. — sman-sgám (sman sgam) medicine-box. — sman-mčód (sman mchod) the best, or a very superior medicine Pth. — sman-ljoṅs (sman ljongs) a country rich in medicinal plants. — sman-rtá (sman rta) the vehicle or substance in which medicine is taken Med. — sman-snod (sman snod) medicine glass or vessel. — smán-pa (sman pa) physician Dzl., Glr., Med. — smán-dpe (sman dpe) medical book. — sman-dpyád (sman dpyad) v. dpyád-pa (dpyad pa). — sman-blá (sman bla) v. above. II. the same as, or something like klu (klu) Glr., Mil. III. Lex. = paṅ (pang); Sch. also has: sman-séms (sman sems) a beneficent mind, a mind intent on working good'. IV. incorr. for dman (dman).
1. sbst, ready money, gen. smar-rkyáṅ (smar rkyang); zoṅ miṅ smar (zong min smar) money, and not goods Lex. — 2. vb. careless and incorr. pronunciation of smrá-ba (smra ba).
n. of a lunar mansion v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar).
mirage Lex. = मरीचि; prob. also a reflection in water, čur-°krul-smig-rgyú (cur 'krul smig rgyu).
provinc. for smyúg-ma (smyug ma) cane, reed Do.

1. also skár-ma-smin-drúg (skar ma smin drug) कार्त्तिक, the Pleiades; smin-drug-zlá-ba (smin drug zla ba) the month in which the moon standing near the Pleiades is full, Oct. or Nov., Glr.; smin-drug-bú (smin drug bu), कार्त्तिकेय, the son of Siva, god of war Lex. — 2. Pur. Eremurus spectabilis, v. bre (bre).

I. (विपाक) to ripen, ripeness, maturity; most frq. ripe, °brás-bu smín-no ('bras bu smin no) B., smín soṅ (smin song) vulgo, the fruit is ripe; smín-par gyúr-ba (smin par gyur ba) Glr. smin °óṅ-ba (smin 'ong ba) to ripen; the growing on to maturity of an animal germ; also the 'stadium maturationis', or the full development of a disease Med.; applied to conversion Pth. and elsewh.; rgyud smín-čiṅ gról-bar byín-gyis rlobs (rgyud smin ching grol bar byin gyis rlobs) give them the benediction for being saved (absorbed into Nirvana) after having attained to maturity of mind Mil.; smin-gról-la (smin grol la) or smin-gról-gyi lám-la gód-pa (smin grol gyi lam la god pa) to lead to conversion and salvation Glr.; rnám-par smín-pa (rnam par smin pa) v. rnám-pa (rnam pa). — smin-grol-glíṅ (smin grol gling) n. of a monastery Cs. II. Bal. to give (sbyín-pa (sbyin pa)).
eye-brow, smín(-mai) dbrag (smin(-ma'i) dbrag) Med., smin-p'rag (smin phrag) Mil., smin-mtsams (smin mtsams) Glr. the space between the eye-brows.

1. sbst. a disease, acc. to Cs. = °dus-nád ('dus nad), v. °dú-ba ('du ba), 1. — 2. adj. dark bay, cherry-brown, purple-brown; *gya-múg* (*gya mug*) C. violet coloured; dmar-smug (dmar smug) brownish white Wdn.; smug-smúg (smug smug) Sch. dark red. — smug-rtsí (smug rtsi) 1. red colour, with which sacrificial utensils are painted Lex. — 2. Macrotomia, a plant with dark-red root, used for dyeing, smug-tsós (smug tsos) paint or colour yielded by this plant Cs.
1. v. rme-ba (rme ba). — 2. rtsís-kyi smé-ba (rtsis kyi sme ba) Lex. a kind of arithmetical figure in geomancy, which is used together with the Chinese diagrams, spar-k'a (spar kha) Mil.

pf. and imp. smos (smos), not frq., yet in some cases of constant use, for smrá-ba (smra ba) to say, miṅ-nas smó-ba (ming nas smo ba) to call by name, to name Do.; . . . žes smós-pa (zhes smos pa) the assertion that . . . Wdn.; goṅ-du smós-pa (gong du smos pa) above-mentioned Do.; lta či smos (lta ci smos) v. lta (lta).
